name: Turkey Potpies Baked in Mini Squashes
recipeIngredient: 4 Mini Winter Squash (such as Sweet Dumplings), cut into 4-6 inches in diameter
1/3 cup Green Beans, cut into 1/2 inch pieces
1 cup Cooked Turkey, cubed and skinned
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut off the top third of each squash. Thoroughly scrape out seeds and loose fiber. Place tops back on squash and place them on a baking sheet. Bake at 350°F until flesh is tender, about 30 minutes.
2. Bring broth to boil in a pan. Add carrots, celery, green beans, and sweet red pepper and blanch until tender-crisp. Reserve vegetables and drain off remaining stock into a large measuring cup. Add enough milk to make 1 1/2 cups of stock mixture.
3. Melt butter in a heavy saucepan over medium heat. Add onion and mushrooms and sauté until soft, about 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in flour and cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in stock mixture until ingredients are well incorporated. Add herbs and seasonings and cook over low heat for 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in reserved vegetables and turkey. Remove from heat and keep warm.
4. Prepare biscuits (recipe below).
5. When Squash is done, remove from oven and increase temperature to 400°F. Trim off excess flesh around edges of squash to create space for filling. Use extra squash flesh as filler. Spoon filling into each squash. Top each with an unbaked biscuit round. Place on baking sheet and bake until biscuits are done, about 20 minutes. Makes 4 servings.
Note: Fresh herbs can be substituted for dried herbs, using twice the amount.
